name: fixture-03-cto-saas-scaleup
description: VP Engineering at a Series C vertical SaaS (logistics) applying for CTO at a Series B horizontal SaaS (developer tools). Close fit on scaling + hiring; stretch is multi-product + dev-tool specific instinct.

resume_data.name: Elena Rodriguez
key_achievements: Scaled engineering from 20 to 120 across 3 offices over 3 years while holding attrition below 7% and maintaining a 4.6 Glassdoor eng rating.
Re-architected the single-tenant monolith into a multi-tenant platform during a revenue-quadrupling window — zero migration-induced outages across the 800-customer rollout.
Owned the technical diligence process for the Series C ($95M) and a $40M strategic investment, including 9 architecture deep-dives with investor-side principals.

requirements: 8+ years of engineering leadership, including one post-product-market-fit scaling motion
Hands-on architecture credibility with the engineering team
Developer-tooling or API-first SaaS experience strongly preferred
Track record of shipping high-quality developer UX at scale
Comfort representing technology to investors, partners, and enterprise buyers
culture_cues: engineer-first — the CEO still writes code on weekends
asynchronous by default — written memos over meetings
product-led growth, not enterprise sales motion

letter_plan.opening_hook: Lead with the 20→120 scaling run — the exact post-PMF motion the JD describes — to plant credibility before addressing the dev-tool specificity the JD flags.
body_points: Scaling + attrition + Glassdoor numbers aren't generic — they're specifically the engineer-first culture Ironloom has; cite the async-communication habits from a 3-office org as evidence this culture fit is real, not claimed.

Monolith-to-multi-tenant rewrite with zero outages demonstrates the hands-on architecture credibility the JD flags; address the dev-tool specificity directly — API-first patterns from the logistics platform transfer, but acknowledge the DX craft (docs, SDK, samples) is something to learn on the job alongside the engineering org.

Series C + $40M diligence work is direct proof of the investor-representation requirement; reframe enterprise partner conversations as the pattern that also translates to developer-community ecosystem partnerships.
letter_plan.closing_strategy: Acknowledge the dev-tool domain transition as the one real stretch, and frame the candidate's operator bias as the reason they'd be credible fast — the eng-first culture and async memos are where this person already operates.
